creator: junchoon@dec.sakura.ne.jp
text:
(In reply to Michael from comment #9)
Are you still using any nvidia card that requires 390 series of driver?
If yes, I could find 390.157 on nvidia official Unix drivers page.

  https://www.nvidia.com/en-us/drivers/details/196217/

As there's no contents for "Release Highlights" part and I'm not a nvidia-insider, I cannot know what had been changed since 390.154, though, at /usr/ports/x11/nvidia-driver and /usr/ports/x11/linux-nvidia-libs with latest 570.124.04 (not updated for 570.133.07) state on main (aka latest) branch,
  # make DISTVERSION=390.157 -DNO_CHECKSUM fetch
  # make DISTVERSION=390.157 -DNO_CHECKSUM
  # make DISTVERSION=390.157 -DNO_CHECKSUM package
went fine (not installed, as I'm on 570.133.07 with overriding version now) on stable/14, amd64.

This is the most straightforward version overriding (doesn't work for graphics/nvidia-drm-[510|515|61|66]-kmod, though).

As I no longer have "working" stable/13 and i386 environment, cannot test for 13.x (amd64/i386) and/or stable/14, i386. So if 390 series is still needed and 390.157 works fine for you, I can trivially create patch for it (not sure it's accepted or not, though). But after ongoing 470.256.02 is accepted and landed.
